acceptance_criteria: No metadata inconsistency about semantic scoring remains.
All audit numbers in the memo have sheet!cell provenance.
Sensitivity table and scenario cell links exist and match memo claims.
Monitoring plan and limit4 resolution actions are documented.
verification_steps_for_reviewer: Open README sheet and confirm sheet!cell links exist for each audit value.
Open sensitivity sheet and confirm -5% scenario formulas reference base release cells and recompute values.
Confirm metadata fields consistently state whether semantic grading was performed.

planned_changes.description: Add a README sheet in reservoir_rule_curve.xlsx listing exact sheet!cell addresses for: input columns, intermediate audit rows (storage term, inflow term, release term, shortage term), normalized_risk cell, and classification cell. Mirror those sheet!cell links in drought_policy.md next to each record line.
planned_changes.verification_criteria: All audit values in drought_policy.md include sheet!cell links that when inspected in the XLSX file point to formulas/values producing the memo numbers.

planned_changes.description: Implement explicit +/-5% scenario formulas in a sensitivity sheet with cells that reference the base release cell and compute the perturbed normalized_risk; add a small table showing baseline and -5% risk values and whether classification flips occurred.
planned_changes.verification_criteria: Sensitivity sheet contains cell formulas referencing base release cells and a table showing recomputed risk values that match memo claims.

planned_changes.description: Update metadata and any grader/verifier-related outputs to remove contradictory semantic score claims or, if semantic evaluation was performed, update deterministic_precheck metadata to reflect that and provide a short note describing the semantic checks performed.
planned_changes.verification_criteria: No internal inconsistency remains: deterministic_precheck and raw_model_output/raw_verifier_output consistently state whether semantic grading was performed and reflect matching scores.

planned_changes.description: Document threshold closed/open convention (exact equality handling) in memo and add a 'monitoring & re-eval' block describing telemetry fields to check, re-evaluation cadence after a release trim, and criteria for rescinding trims.
planned_changes.verification_criteria: Memo contains explicit threshold rule (e.g., risk >1.15 => fail; risk ==1.15 => warn/pass convention indicated) and a monitoring plan with referenced spreadsheet cells.

planned_changes.description: In the memo, keep the 'needs_review' flag but add a recommended method to resolve limit4 for shortage_risk (suggested data source or stakeholder step) and, if appropriate, include an alternative defensible defaulting policy with justification.
planned_changes.verification_criteria: Memo documents a concrete next step to obtain limit4 or a defensible default policy and marks which records remain provisional.

planned_changes.description: Attach a minimal CSV of the four records and a short verification script or manual verification steps so reviewers without openpyxl can recompute the memo values easily.
planned_changes.verification_criteria: CSV + script produce the same normalized_risk values reported in the memo when executed in a standard Python environment.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Deliverables are complete and numerically consistent with the memo's audit rows; sensitivity and recommendation logic are reasonable and within budget. Two issues should be addressed before final acceptance: (1) an internal inconsistency flagged by the verifier where the deterministic precheck says semantic correctness was not evaluated but later outputs assert semantic scores of 1.0; (2) limited machine-verifiability because openpyxl was unavailable and the workbook lacks explicit sheet/cell provenance and explicit +/-5% scenario cell links.
